perturbation.original_text: This agreement is entered into as of ??/??/???? b y and between: (1) COM PANY A FULL NAM E a com pany duly incorporat ed under t he laws of Engl and, wi th a regi stration number ???????? ( “COMPANY A”); and (2) COM PANY B FULL NAM E a com pany duly incorporat ed under t he laws of Engl and, wi th a regi stration number ???????? ( “COMPANY B”).
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement is entered into as of ??/??/???? b y and between: (1) COM PANY A FULL NAM E a com pany duly incorporat ed under t he laws of Engl and, wi th a regi stration number ???????? ( “COMPANY A”); and (2) COM PANY B FULL NAM E a com pany ( “COMPANY B”).
perturbation.explanation: Removing the text 'duly incorporated under t he laws of Engl and, wi th a regi stration number ????????' from Company B makes it difficult to verify its legal standing. This could become a liability if Company B were to breach the contract or act illegally, creating a legal contradiction.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 15 of the Companies Act 2006 requires companies to be properly registered and have a registration number. By removing this information from the contract, the NDA fails to properly identify a party to the agreement, thus introducing potential legal risk and contradiction to company law.

perturbation.justification: The omission of 'duly incorporated under the laws of England, with a registration number ????????' from Company B's description directly impacts its verifiable legal standing as required by company law. While Scraped Law Snippet 2 is unavailable, Scraped Law Snippet 1, referencing the Companies Act 2006, supports the claim that the altered text contradicts legal principles. The removal obstructs the identification of Company B as a properly registered entity under the laws of England, therefore the modification undermines legal compliance and creates a legally significant contradiction. The Law Explanation supports this assertion, stating that Section 15 of the Companies Act 2006 necessitates proper registration and a registration number for companies, requirements undermined by the omission in the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: Each part y shall hold in trust and confi dence and prot ect and treat as strictly secret and confidential at all times the Inform ation received from the other party, and in respect of su ch Inform ation, each party agrees that it: (a) shall not directly o r indirectly d isclose, verbally, in writin g or in any other form, or allow the Inform ation to be di sclosed to any other person, fi rm or com pany; (b) shall keep secret and shall not use, m anipulate or exploit the Information, except for the Purpose and then only in strict accordan ce with the Purpose and the terms of this Agreem ent; (c) shall restrict disclosure of th e Inform ation to such of i ts employees or consul tants or subcont ractors who need t o know for t he Purpose and shal l procure t hat such persons observe ful ly the provi sions of this Ag reem ent as if th ey were th emselves parties to it; (d) shall not copy , store or di stribute any Inform ation or al low any Inform ation to be copi ed, st ored or distributed except for such copi es, st orage or di stribution as are st rictly requi red for t he Purpose;
perturbation.changed_text: Each part y shall hold in trust and confi dence and prot ect and treat as strictly secret and confidential at all times the Inform ation received from the other party, and in respect of su ch Inform ation, each party agrees that it: (a) shall not directly o r indirectly d isclose, verbally, in writin g or in any other form, or allow the Inform ation to be di sclosed to any other person, fi rm or com pany; (b) shall keep secret and shall not use, m anipulate or exploit the Information, except for the Purpose and then only in strict accordan ce with the Purpose and the terms of this Agreem ent; (c) shall restrict disclosure of th e Inform ation to such of i ts employees or consul tants or subcont ractors who need t o know for t he Purpose; (d) shall not copy , store or di stribute any Inform ation or al low any Inform ation to be copi ed, st ored or distributed except for such copi es, st orage or di stribution as are st rictly requi red for t he Purpose;
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'and shal l procure t hat such persons observe ful ly the provi sions of this Ag reem ent as if th ey were th emselves parties to it;' the company no longer has an explicit requirement to ensure that employees, consultants, or subcontractors are bound by the NDA. This weakens the protection of confidential information under UK law.
perturbation.law_explanation: Regulation 4(2) of the Trade Secrets (Enforcement, etc.) Regulations 2018 emphasizes the need for reasonable steps to keep information secret. Removing the requirement to bind employees and subcontractors weakens these reasonable steps, potentially jeopardizing trade secret protection.

perturbation.justification: The original contract requires the parties to ensure that their employees, consultants, and subcontractors also adhere to the confidentiality agreement. The changed text removes this obligation. According to the Law Explanation, Regulation 4(2) of the Trade Secrets (Enforcement, etc.) Regulations 2018 emphasizes the need for reasonable steps to keep information secret. By removing the requirement to ensure third parties are bound by the NDA, the contract weakens the steps taken to protect trade secrets, potentially violating Regulation 4(2).

perturbation.original_text: shall protect the com mercial inte rest of the disclosing party and sh all take the sam e precautions to protect the Inform ation as a careful and prudent person woul d be expect ed to employ for i ts own business and confi dential inform ation and, i n doing so, shal l use reasonabl e care t o protect the Inform ation from unaut horised use, harm , expl oitation, m anipulation, m odification, interference, misuse, m isappropri ation, copy or di sclosure what soever, except as specifica lly au thorised by the disclosing part y in writing; (f) shall keep all In formation that is electronically stored in a tangible form segregat ed from all of its own propert y and i n a safe and secure envi ronm ent and protect and keep safe all such Inform ation from any loss, harm , theft, unaut horised use, t ampering, sabot age, unaut horised dupl ication, dest ruction, damage or interference whatsoever
perturbation.changed_text: shall protect the com mercial inte rest of the disclosing party and sh all take reasonabl e steps to protect the Inform ation from unaut horised use, harm , expl oitation, m anipulation, m odification, interference, misuse, m isappropri ation, copy or di sclosure what soever, except as specifica lly au thorised by the disclosing part y in writing; (f) shall keep all In formation that is electronically stored segregat ed from all of its own propert y and protect and keep safe all such Inform ation from any loss, harm , theft, unaut horised use, t ampering, sabot age, unaut horised dupl ication, dest ruction, damage or interference whatsoever
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'same precautions to protect the Information as a careful and prudent person woul d be expect ed to employ for i ts own business and confi dential inform ation' to 'reasonabl e steps to protect the Information' and deleting the phrase 'in a tangible form' and 'in a safe and secure environment' from section (f), the contract reduces the standard of care and security required to protect confidential information. This introduces a legal contradiction.
perturbation.law_explanation: Regulation 3(2) of the Trade Secrets (Enforcement, etc.) Regulations 2018 refers to 'reasonable steps' taken by the trade secret holder to keep information confidential. The modifications weaken the definition of 'reasonable steps'.

perturbation.justification: The original contract requires the party to take 'the same precautions to protect the Information as a careful and prudent person would be expected to employ for its own business and confidential information' and to keep electronically stored information 'in a safe and secure environment'. The modified contract weakens this obligation by changing the standard of care to 'reasonable steps' and omitting the requirement to keep electronically stored information 'in a safe and secure environment'. Although the LLM states that the modification weakens the definition of reasonable steps, the snipped provided does not explain the strength of this legal contradiction. However, given that the Omission Legal Contradiction is defined by the removal of essential information creating legal loopholes and removing a legally mandated consumer protection, and that the original contract text had essential language protecting confidential information and ensuring a high degree of protection (prudent person standard), the omission of that language is a legal loophole that reduces the consumer protection originally agreed upon, and thus is a legally mandated consumer protection. Thus, the changes create a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: shall, upon dem and, inform the disclosing part y of the location of t he Inform ation and t he measures that it h as taken to preserv e its co nfidentiality; (h) shall retu rn to the disclosing party all In formation, copi es and t angible manifestations t hereof, i n any form whatsoever, an d delete all In formation from all retriev al system s and databases o r destroy the same immediately upon t he direction of t he disclosing part y, and furni sh to the disclosing part y, immediately upon i ts request , a cert ificate of a seni or offi cer or di rector attesting to such ret urn, deletion or dest ruction, as t he case m ay be;
perturbation.changed_text: shall, upon dem and, inform the disclosing part y of the location of t he Inform ation; (h) shall retu rn to the disclosing party all In formation, copi es and t angible manifestations t hereof, i n any form whatsoever, an d delete all In formation from all retriev al system s and databases o r destroy the same immediately upon t he direction of t he disclosing part y;
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'and t he measures that it h as taken to preserv e its co nfidentiality' and 'and furni sh to the disclosing part y, immediately upon i ts request , a cert ificate of a seni or offi cer or di rector attesting to such ret urn, deletion or dest ruction, as t he case m ay be;' reduces the accountability of the receiving party and weakens the disclosing party's ability to enforce the NDA and verify compliance with UK law related to data protection and trade secrets.
perturbation.law_explanation: Schedule 2, Part 1, 6(1) of the Data Protection Act 2018 emphasizes the need for appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure data security. Removing the requirements for the receiving party to provide information on confidentiality measures and a certificate of destruction weakens the disclosing party's ability to verify those measures.
perturbation.justification: The omission of 'and the measures that it has taken to preserve its confidentiality' and 'and furni sh to the disclosing part y, immediately upon i ts request , a cert ificate of a seni or offi cer or di rector attesting to such ret urn, deletion or dest ruction, as t he case m ay be' creates a legal contradiction. The modified text weakens the disclosing party's ability to verify compliance and enforce the NDA, and reduces the accountability of the receiving party. Although there are no scraped snippets, the Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation support this conclusion.
